constraints: Reveal rooms, corridors, and connections through exploration
Do not reveal undiscovered secrets automatically
Keep objective guidance light
Avoid GPS-style navigation
Target players who enjoy skill-based top-down combat
Provide many meaningfully tailored difficulty settings for casual through mastery-focused players
Do not rely only on simple health or damage multipliers to differentiate difficulty
The complete MVP campaign, saves, progression, and difficulty modes must work offline
Optional online systems must not make the core game connectivity-dependent
Multiplayer is post-MVP and should follow only after a successful MVP
Preserve clean extension boundaries for later online and multiplayer systems without implementing their operational complexity in the MVP
Real-time cooperative campaign-style play is the first major multiplayer direction after a successful MVP
Co-op should support shared combat, exploration, objectives, and bosses
Leaderboards are also desired later but remain post-MVP
Do not implement multiplayer networking, multiplayer scaling, shared progression, or leaderboard services in the MVP
Avoid unnecessary single-player-only hard-coding where clean extension boundaries are practical
Release the polished MVP for Windows PC first
Support keyboard and mouse plus gamepad in the MVP
Android is the next intended platform after the PC version is stable
Keep gameplay actions platform-neutral and UI scalable
Suspend saves and data-driven content should remain compatible with later mobile expansion
Touch controls, mobile performance validation, and app-store release work are post-MVP

recovered_state.rule: Re-verify reconstructed details before final requirements

open_questions: Exact level size and expected session length
Stack and technical architecture after product scope is mature

readiness.reason: High-weight Product Discovery decisions and recovery verification remain incomplete

next_action.instruction: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting the intended MVP level size and expected first-clear versus repeat-run session length. Put the agent recommendation after all options at the end of the card. Persist the accepted answer and update the handoff before continuing.
